name: Aqara MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Aqara (aqara org) for their smart home ecosystem. Enables AI agents to control Aqara smart home devices — lights, sensors, locks, cameras, thermostats — via the Aqara cloud API. Supports scenes, automations, and device state queries across the Aqara device lineup.
use_cases: Controlling Aqara smart home devices from home automation agents
Querying device states and sensor readings from monitoring agents
Triggering Aqara scenes and automations from AI assistants
Building AI-driven home automation workflows on top of Aqara infrastructure
not_for: Teams using other smart home platforms (Philips Hue, Nest, SmartThings) without Aqara
Local-only smart home control (requires Aqara cloud connectivity)
Industrial IoT or commercial building management (consumer device focus)
best_when: A smart home enthusiast or developer using Aqara devices wants AI agents to control and automate their home environment through natural language or programmatic workflows.
avoid_when: You don't have Aqara devices. Aqara's ecosystem is strong for Zigbee/Matter/HomeKit but if you use other ecosystems, look for platform-specific MCPs.

auth.notes: Aqara OAuth 2.0 via Aqara developer platform. Requires Aqara developer account and app registration. OAuth tokens grant access to linked Aqara Home.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for personal use via Aqara developer program
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API access; hardware costs apply
pricing.notes: Aqara cloud API is free for personal use. Developer program registration required. MCP server is free open source from Aqara official org.

performance.rate_limits: Aqara API rate limits apply; typically adequate for home automation use

known_agent_gotchas: PHYSICAL EFFECTS: Controls real physical devices — test thoroughly before autonomous agent use
Aqara cloud connectivity required — local-only control not supported through this server
Device commands may have propagation delays depending on hub and network conditions
OAuth token refresh needed — implement token management for long-running agents
agent_readiness.security_notes: PHYSICAL DEVICE CONTROL: Controls real home devices including locks and security sensors. Restrict agent permissions carefully. OAuth via Aqara cloud. Data includes home layout and device states — sensitive for privacy.

security_score_components.security_notes: Physical device control including locks. OAuth authorization required. Home data is privacy-sensitive. Restrict to read-only or specific device types for agent use.
